growth_records.strain: metagenome-assembled genomes (MAG cluster), no isolate strains
growth_records.medium: 13C-bicarbonate + thiosulfate amended Hainich CZE groundwater microcosm (well H41)
growth_records.cite: Taubert, Martin et al. Bolstering fitness via CO2 fixation and organic carbon uptake: mixotrophs in modern groundwater. The ISME Journal (2022). DOI:10.1038/s41396-021-01163-x. [PMC8941145]
growth_records.snippet: By comparing the signal intensities of 12C- and 13C-enriched peptides, the generation time of these autotrophs was determined to be <2 days (Fig. 3), highlighting the rapid production of new 13C-labeled biomass from 13CO2.
growth_records.notes: Generation time reported as <2 days (fell below the 2-day detection limit, bdl). Values are a LOWER BOUND on growth rate: mu > ln2/(2*24 h) = 0.693147/48 = 0.014441 h-1; doubling_time_h reported <=48 (48 used as upper bound). Cluster-level (multiple unnamed autotroph MAGs), environmental microcosm; true mu is faster than the tabulated value.
